# SECTION 3: Step-by-Step Derivations & Analytical Matrix

## 3.1 Detailed Worked Example — Land Use Polygon Area Calculation

> [!NOTE]
> **Problem:** A residential polygon digitized in QGIS has the following 5 vertices in UTM Zone 43N (meters): $V_1(500000, 1000000)$, $V_2(500120, 1000000)$, $V_3(500150, 1000080)$, $V_4(500100, 1000150)$, $V_5(500000, 1000100)$. Compute its area in hectares.

### Step 1 — List the vertices cyclically
$$(x_1, y_1) = (500000, 1000000)$$
$$(x_2, y_2) = (500120, 1000000)$$
$$(x_3, y_3) = (500150, 1000080)$$
$$(x_4, y_4) = (500100, 1000150)$$
$$(x_5, y_5) = (500000, 1000100)$$
$$(x_6, y_6) = (x_1, y_1) = (500000, 1000000) \quad \text{(closure — repeat V1)}$$

### Step 2 — Apply the Shoelace formula

$$A = \frac{1}{2} \left| \sum_{i=1}^{n} (x_i y_{i+1} - x_{i+1} y_i) \right|$$

### Step 3 — Compute each term $(x_i y_{i+1} - x_{i+1} y_i)$

**Term 1:** $x_1 y_2 - x_2 y_1 = (500000)(1000000) - (500120)(1000000)$

$$= 500000 \times 10^6 - 500120 \times 10^6 = -120 \times 10^6$$

**Term 2:** $x_2 y_3 - x_3 y_2 = (500120)(1000080) - (500150)(1000000)$

$$= 500120 \times 1000080 - 500150 \times 10^6$$
$$= 5.00240 \times 10^{11} - 5.00150 \times 10^{11} = 9.0 \times 10^{7}$$

**Term 3:** $x_3 y_4 - x_4 y_3 = (500150)(1000150) - (500100)(1000080)$

$$= 500150 \times 1000150 - 500100 \times 1000080$$
$$= 5.00400 \times 10^{11} - 5.00300 \times 10^{11} = 1.0 \times 10^{8}$$

**Term 4:** $x_4 y_5 - x_5 y_4 = (500100)(1000100) - (500000)(1000150)$

$$= 500100 \times 1000100 - 500000 \times 1000150$$
$$= 5.00300 \times 10^{11} - 5.00075 \times 10^{11} = 2.25 \times 10^{7}$$

**Term 5:** $x_5 y_1 - x_1 y_5 = (500000)(1000000) - (500000)(1000100)$

$$= 5.0 \times 10^{11} - 5.0005 \times 10^{11} = -5.0 \times 10^{7}$$

### Step 4 — Sum all terms

$$\sum = -120 \times 10^6 + 90 \times 10^6 + 100 \times 10^6 + 22.5 \times 10^6 - 50 \times 10^6$$

$$= \frac{(-120 + 90 + 100 + 22.5 - 50) \times 10^6}{1}$$

$$= 42.5 \times 10^6$$

### Step 5 — Apply the absolute value and divide by 2

$$A = \frac{1}{2} \left| 42.5 \times 10^6 \right| = 21.25 \times 10^6 \text{ m}^2$$

### Step 6 — Convert to hectares

$$A_{\text{ha}} = \frac{21.25 \times 10^6}{10{,}000} = 2125.0 \text{ ha}$$

$$\boxed{A = 21.25 \text{ km}^2 = 2125.0 \text{ ha}}$$

## 3.2 Analytical Matrix — Classification Decision Rules

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Anderson Level I LULC Classes — Spectral Interpretation Guide (Sentinel-2 Bands)**

| LULC Class | Spectral Signature (B4-Red, B8-NIR, B11-SWIR) | Texture / Pattern | Field Validation Clue |
|------------|-----------------------------------------------|-------------------|------------------------|
| **Built-up** | High B4, B11; Low B8 | Geometric, sharp edges | Concrete, rooftops visible |
| **Agriculture** | Medium B8; High B11 in fallow | Strip / patchwork | Field boundaries |
| **Forest** | High B8, High B11; Low B4 | Rough, uniform texture | Canopy closure |
| **Water Body** | Very low B4, B8, B11 | Smooth, dark | Reflectance in NIR ~0 |
| **Barren Land** | High B4, B11; Low B8 | Smooth, featureless | Soil exposure |
| **Wetland** | Low B4; High B8, B11 | Mottled, mixed | Standing water + vegetation |
| **Snow / Ice** | Very high B4, B8, B11 | Smooth, bright | High elevation only |

## 3.3 Worked Georeferencing RMSE Example

> [!NOTE]
> **Problem:** Five GCPs were used to georeference a satellite image. Compute the RMSE.

| GCP | $X_{\text{obs}}$ | $X_{\text{true}}$ | $Y_{\text{obs}}$ | $Y_{\text{true}}$ | $\Delta X$ | $\Delta Y$ | $\Delta X^2 + \Delta Y^2$ |
|-----|------|--------|------|--------|------|------|------|
| 1 | 500012 | 500010 | 1000005 | 1000000 | 2 | 5 | 29 |
| 2 | 500115 | 500120 | 1000098 | 1000100 | -5 | -2 | 29 |
| 3 | 500205 | 500200 | 1000205 | 1000200 | 5 | 5 | 50 |
| 4 | 500310 | 500300 | 1000308 | 1000300 | 10 | 8 | 164 |
| 5 | 500402 | 500400 | 1000400 | 1000400 | 2 | 0 | 4 |

**Sum of squared residuals:**

$$\sum (\Delta X^2 + \Delta Y^2) = 29 + 29 + 50 + 164 + 4 = 276$$

**RMSE computation:**

$$\text{RMSE} = \sqrt{\frac{276}{5}} = \sqrt{55.2} = 7.43 \text{ m}$$

**Interpretation:** For a Sentinel-2 image at **10 m native resolution**, an RMSE of **7.43 m (< 1 pixel)** qualifies as **sub-pixel accuracy** — meeting KTU lab acceptance criteria. ✅

# SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 PART A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question 1 (3 Marks)
**`[KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]`** Define **Land Use / Land Cover (LULC)**. List any **four** Level-I LULC classes as per the **Anderson Classification System**.

**Model Answer:**

> **Land Use / Land Cover (LULC)** refers to the categorization of the Earth's surface into distinct biophysical and anthropogenic categories based on its functional use (land use) and observable surface characteristics (land cover). It is a foundational geospatial dataset used in urban planning, hydrology, and environmental engineering.

**Four Level-I LULC Classes (Anderson System):**
1. **Built-up Land** — residential, commercial, industrial areas
2. **Agricultural Land** — cropland, fallow, plantation
3. **Forest Land** — dense, open, degraded
4. **Water Bodies** — rivers, reservoirs, tanks
5. *(Alternative: Wetlands, Barren Land, Snow/Ice)*

---

### Question 2 (3 Marks)
**`[KTU University Exam - May 2024]`** What is **georeferencing**? State the minimum number of **GCPs (Ground Control Points)** required and the acceptable RMSE threshold.

**Model Answer:**

> **Georeferencing** is the process of assigning real-world ground coordinates to a raster image (e.g., a scanned toposheet or satellite image) so that it aligns with a known coordinate reference system, enabling it to be overlaid accurately with other spatial datasets.
>
> - **Minimum GCPs:** **4** (for a 1st-order polynomial); **8–12** recommended for higher accuracy.
> - **Acceptable RMSE:** **< 0.5 pixel (sub-pixel accuracy)**
> - **Standard Datum:** WGS 1984 / UTM Projection

---

## 5.2 PART B — Full Descriptive Questions (14 Marks Each, Internal Choice)

> [!NOTE]
> **KTU 2024 Pattern:** Each Part B question carries 14 marks, divided into two sub-parts of 7 marks each. Candidates must answer **either** Question A **or** Question B (full internal choice).

---

### **Question A (14 Marks)**
**`[KTU University Exam - Dec 2023 Model Paper]`**

**(a)** Explain in detail the **step-by-step methodology** for preparing a **Land Use / Land Cover map** from satellite imagery using GIS. List the input data sources, software tools, classification system, and final deliverables. **(7 Marks)**

**(b)** A digitized **irregular agricultural polygon** has the following vertices in UTM coordinates (meters):  
$V_1(1000, 2000)$, $V_2(1200, 2050)$, $V_3(1300, 2200)$, $V_4(1100, 2300)$, $V_5(950, 2150)$.  
Compute the **area** in square meters and **hectares** using the Shoelace formula. **(7 Marks)**

---

#### Model Solution for Question A(a) — 7 Marks

**Step 1 — Data Acquisition (1 Mark):**
- Download **Sentinel-2** (10 m multispectral) or **LISS-IV** (5.8 m) imagery from **Bhuvan** or **USGS Earth Explorer**.
- Acquire **SoI toposheet** at 1:25,000 scale.
- Obtain **cadastral / village boundary** from revenue records.

**Step 2 — Pre-Processing (1 Mark):**
- Apply **radiometric and atmospheric correction** (e.g., DOS1 in QGIS).
- **Mosaic and subset** to the Area of Interest (AOI).

**Step 3 — Georeferencing (1 Mark):**
- Identify **8–12 GCPs** at stable landmarks (road intersections).
- Apply **2nd-order polynomial transformation**.
- Verify **RMSE < 0.5 pixel**.

**Step 4 — On-Screen Digitization (1.5 Marks):**
- Open georeferenced image in **QGIS / ArcGIS**.
- Create a new **polygon shapefile** with LULC fields.
- Trace boundaries for each class maintaining **MMU = 0.25 ha**.
- Use **Anderson Level I (or NRSC 23-class)** scheme.

**Step 5 — Attribute Population (1 Mark):**
- Populate fields: `LULC_Class`, `LULC_Code`, `Area_ha`, `Perimeter_m`, `Source`, `Date`.
- Auto-calculate area using GIS geometry tools.

**Step 6 — Topology & Validation (1 Mark):**
- Run **Check Geometry / Topology** rules.
- Eliminate slivers, fix overshoots/undershoots, fill gaps.
- Cross-validate with **Google Earth** and field photos.

**Step 7 — Final Output (0.5 Mark):**
- Export as **Shapefile (.shp)** and **GeoJSON**.
- Compose final map with **legend, north arrow, scale bar, title block**.

---

#### Model Solution for Question A(b) — 7 Marks

**Vertices (closure required — repeat $V_1$):**

| $i$ | $x_i$ | $y_i$ | $x_i y_{i+1}$ | $x_{i+1} y_i$ |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 1000 | 2000 | $1000 \times 2050 = 2{,}050{,}000$ | $1200 \times 2000 = 2{,}400{,}000$ |
| 2 | 1200 | 2050 | $1200 \times 2200 = 2{,}640{,}000$ | $1300 \times 2050 = 2{,}665{,}000$ |
| 3 | 1300 | 2200 | $1300 \times 2300 = 2{,}990{,}000$ | $1100 \times 2200 = 2{,}420{,}000$ |
| 4 | 1100 | 2300 | $1100 \times 2150 = 2{,}365{,}000$ | $950 \times 2300 = 2{,}185{,}000$ |
| 5 | 950 | 2150 | $950 \times 2000 = 1{,}900{,}000$ | $1000 \times 2150 = 2{,}150{,}000$ |
| 6 | 1000 | 2000 | *(closure)* | *(closure)* |

**Sum of products:**

$$\sum x_i y_{i+1} = 2{,}050{,}000 + 2{,}640{,}000 + 2{,}990{,}000 + 2{,}365{,}000 + 1{,}900{,}000 = 11{,}945{,}000$$

$$\sum x_{i+1} y_i = 2{,}400{,}000 + 2{,}665{,}000 + 2{,}420{,}000 + 2{,}185{,}000 + 2{,}150{,}000 = 11{,}820{,}000$$

**Apply Shoelace formula:**

$$A = \frac{1}{2} \left| 11{,}945{,}000 - 11{,}820{,}000 \right| = \frac{1}{2} \times 125{,}000 = 62{,}500 \text{ m}^2$$

**Convert to hectares:**

$$A_{\text{ha}} = \frac{62{,}500}{10{,}000} = 6.25 \text{ ha}$$

$$\boxed{A = 62{,}500 \text{ m}^2 = 6.25 \text{ ha}}$$

---

### **Question B (14 Marks) — Alternative Choice**
**`[KTU University Exam - May 2024 Model Paper]`**

**(a)** Discuss the importance of **attribute table design** in LULC data preparation. Design a complete attribute schema for a **municipal LULC database** with at least **8 fields**, justifying each. **(7 Marks)**

**(b)** Five GCPs were observed during georeferencing. Compute the **Total RMSE** and comment on whether the image meets the **sub-pixel accuracy** standard. Pixel size = **10 m**.

| GCP | $X_{\text{obs}}$ (m) | $X_{\text{true}}$ (m) | $Y_{\text{obs}}$ (m) | $Y_{\text{true}}$ (m) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 500005 | 500000 | 1000008 | 1000000 |
| 2 | 500110 | 500100 | 1000105 | 1000100 |
| 3 | 500203 | 500200 | 1000200 | 1000200 |
| 4 | 500312 | 500300 | 1000295 | 1000300 |
| 5 | 500398 | 500400 | 1000402 | 1000400 |

**(7 Marks)**

---

#### Model Solution for Question B(a) — 7 Marks

**Importance of Attribute Table (3 Marks):**
- The attribute table transforms a "dumb" shape into a "smart" GIS object that can be queried, analyzed, and symbolized.
- It enables **spatial queries** (e.g., "show all polygons with LULC_Class = 'Industrial' AND Area_ha > 5").
- It supports **statistical analysis** (e.g., land use composition %, change detection).
- It is the **bridge** between geometry and semantics.

**Proposed Municipal LULC Attribute Schema (4 Marks):**

| # | Field Name | Data Type | Example | Justification |
|---|------------|-----------|---------|---------------|
| 1 | **LULC_ID** | Auto Increment Integer | 1024 | Unique primary key |
| 2 | **LULC_Class** | Text (50) | "Residential" | Anderson Level II classification |
| 3 | **LULC_Code** | Short Integer | 11 | Numeric code for joins |
| 4 | **Area_sqm** | Double | 45320.5 | Auto-computed geometry |
| 5 | **Area_ha** | Double | 4.53 | User-friendly hectare value |
| 6 | **Perimeter_m** | Double | 1024.6 | Boundary length for compactness analysis |
| 7 | **Source_Image** | Text (100) | "Sentinel-2 L2A 2024" | Data provenance |
| 8 | **Date_of_Mapping** | Date | 2024-09-15 | Temporal validity |
| 9 | **Mapper_Name** | Text (50) | "KTU Lab Student" | Accountability |
| 10 | **Remarks** | Text (254) | "Dense cluster" | Free-form notes |

---

#### Model Solution for Question B(b) — 7 Marks

**Compute residuals for each GCP:**

| GCP | $\Delta X$ | $\Delta Y$ | $\Delta X^2$ | $\Delta Y^2$ | $\Delta X^2 + \Delta Y^2$ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | $5$ | $8$ | $25$ | $64$ | $89$ |
| 2 | $10$ | $5$ | $100$ | $25$ | $125$ |
| 3 | $3$ | $0$ | $9$ | $0$ | $9$ |
| 4 | $12$ | $-5$ | $144$ | $25$ | $169$ |
| 5 | $-2$ | $2$ | $4$ | $4$ | $8$ |

**Sum of squared residuals:**

$$\sum (\Delta X^2 + \Delta Y^2) = 89 + 125 + 9 + 169 + 8 = 400$$

**Total RMSE:**

$$\text{RMSE} = \sqrt{\frac{400}{5}} = \sqrt{80} = 8.944 \text{ m}$$

**Comparison with sub-pixel standard (0.5 × 10 m = 5 m):**

$$\text{RMSE} = 8.944 \text{ m} > 5.0 \text{ m} \Rightarrow \text{Fails sub-pixel standard} ❌$$

**Conclusion:** The image **does not meet** the sub-pixel accuracy standard. The student should re-examine GCP placement (especially GCPs 2 and 4 with large residuals) and consider **adding more GCPs** or using a **2nd-order polynomial** transformation.

---

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning — Where Students Lose Marks**
>
> 1. **No Closure in Shoelace Formula** — Forgetting to repeat $V_1$ as $V_{n+1}$ causes **–2 to –3 marks** in numerical questions.
> 2. **Confusing Land Use vs Land Cover** — Land use = *function* (residential, agricultural); Land cover = *physical surface* (concrete, water, vegetation). Examiners dock **1 mark** for interchange.
> 3. **Missing Conversion to Hectares** — Computing area in m² but not converting to ha when asked. Always end with the **boxed final answer in requested units**.
> 4. **Skipping Topology Cleanup** — Examiners expect explicit mention of sliver removal, overshoot fixing, and rule validation for full marks on methodology.
> 5. **No RMSE Comparison to Standard** — Computing RMSE without commenting on whether it meets the **sub-pixel threshold** is considered incomplete. Always conclude with a ✅/❌ verdict.
> 6. **Anderson vs NRSC Confusion** — Anderson is the **international** classification; **NRSC 23-class** is the **Indian standard**. Mention both for full credit.
> 7. **Attribute Table Field Types** — Using "Text" for area fields or "Integer" for class names loses marks. Use **Double** for areas and **Text (String)** for class names.

---

## 5.3 📌 TOPIC RECAP & RAPID REVISION CHECKLIST

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Must-Know Bullet Points Before Walking Into the KTU Lab Exam**

- ✅ **LULC = Land Use + Land Cover** distinction must be clearly stated in every answer.
- ✅ **Anderson Level I (8 classes)** vs **Level II (more detailed)** vs **NRSC 23-class** — know the difference.
- ✅ **Five-stage pipeline:** Acquisition → Pre-processing → Georeferencing → Digitization → Attribute & Topology.
- ✅ **Minimum 4 GCPs** for 1st-order polynomial; **8–12 GCPs** for higher-order; **RMSE < 0.5 pixel** is the sub-pixel gold standard.
- ✅ **Shoelace formula** for polygon area — always close the loop by repeating $V_1$.
- ✅ **$1 \text{ ha} = 10{,}000 \text{ m}^2 = 0.01 \text{ km}^2$** — memorize this conversion cold.
- ✅ **Vector primitives:** Polygon (2D area), Polyline (1D linear), Point (0D discrete).
- ✅ **Topology rules:** No slivers, no overshoots, no undershoots, no gaps, no overlaps.
- ✅ **Standard projections for India:** UTM Zone 43N (mainland Kerala/TN/Karnataka) and WGS 84 datum.
- ✅ **MMU = 0.25 ha (urban) / 1.0 ha (regional)** for LULC granularity.
- ✅ **Attribute table must include:** LULC_Class, LULC_Code, Area_ha, Perimeter_m, Source, Date — minimum 6 fields.
- ✅ **Sources for free satellite data:** Bhuvan (ISRO), USGS Earth Explorer, Copernicus Open Access Hub, ESA Sentinel Hub.
- ✅ **Software tools in KTU labs:** QGIS (free, primary), ArcGIS (licensed, industry standard), Google Earth Pro (validation).
- ✅ **Map composition essentials:** Title, legend, north arrow, scale bar, coordinate grid, data source citation.
- ✅ **Final deliverables for KTU lab record:** Geo-referenced image, vector shapefile, attribute table, classified map PDF, area statistics table.
- ✅ **Downstream engineering uses:** Runoff CN method, master plan, MCDA suitability, change detection, environmental impact assessment.
